behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff/orchestration: collectors + aggregator + summary-call boundary. A unit test of one function is too narrow (MOCK-MISS), but real OS/network is not required (REAL-INFRA no) because in-process fakes for the three source clients can expose missing/overwritten sections. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about input payload composition before generation, not LLM output quality, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/orchestration modules with stubbed source adapters returning distinct markers per source, run one report build, and assert the exact payload sent into summary generation contains all three markers/sections.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience across modules (source adapter error -> orchestrator fallback -> report section rendering). Unit is too low because the bug appears at handoff/state propagation. Real infrastructure is not strictly required: a stub source throwing timeout/connection errors can trigger the same control flow (REAL-INFRA no). No dependence on model judgment (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System: behavior does not require validating transport-specific network semantics, only fallback behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source stubs returning normal data and one stub raising an availability exception; assert process completes, output contains a `data unavailable` note in the correct section, and other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generated narrative fidelity to input values, which is model-output quality (LLM-DEP yes). A deterministic stub would always pass and hide hallucination/omission errors (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration: behavior text targets semantic accuracy of the summary itself, not just routing/retry/payload formatting around the call. Rejected Workflow: only one model decision point is described, not a multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model on representative fixtures with known key metrics/issues/deployments; score whether required key values are preserved correctly (with tolerance policy), and fail on omissions, contradictions, or value distortion.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external delivery/inbox receipt semantics (REAL-INFRA yes: real HTTP/SMTP/API + inbox). In-process mail mocks can hide auth, provider acceptance, formatting, and delivery-path failures (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Agentic: behavior text validates end-to-end delivery to inbox, not summary-quality judgment; model output correctness is not the asserted target.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script end-to-end in a test environment against real/staging data endpoints and real email service credentials, send to a controlled mailbox, then verify inbox receipt, subject/body integrity, and recipient resolution.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is cross-module state handoff: send failure -> local persistence -> next-run retry consumption. This can be reproduced with wired modules, a failing mail adapter stub, and real local filesystem persistence (Integration allows local FS). REAL-INFRA for external network is not mandatory to prove fallback/retry correctness. Rejected System: behavior text does not require validating provider-specific outage mechanics, only correct fallback and retry flow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute run #1 with mail adapter forced to raise unreachable error; assert report file is persisted with retry metadata. Execute run #2 with mail adapter healthy; assert persisted report is retried/sent and local retry artifact is cleared or marked delivered.
